free_text: Proton-Induced Reactions on Nickel with Energies
Between 12 and 45 MeV.
Institut Fuer Kernchemie
.Julic
Degradation of the Proton Energy was Calculated
According to C.F.Williamson Et Al., Cea-R3042(1966).
Due to a Large Angle Straggling of the Protons in the
Stack the Beam Charge Could not be Measured Directly
By Means of a Faraday Cup. Therefore Two Au-Foils
(12.5 MICROMETER EACH) Were Irradiated Under Identical
Conditions, One in Front of the Stack, the
Other in Front of a Faraday Cup. By Comparing the
Activities in Both of these Foils the Total Charge
For the Stack Irradiated was Obtained.
Two Different Stacks were Used For an Energy Range
From 45 to 28 MeV and from 30 to 12 MeV, Respectively.
Each Stack Contained of Foils of Natural Nickel of
High Purity, Thickness 30 Micrometer and of Al-Foils
Of Varying Thicknesses Serving As Energy Degraders.
In Front of Each Stack an Au-Foil of 12.5 Micrometer
Thickness was Mounted For Beam Current Determination.
An Upper Limit of 1% For Products Formed by Secondary
Neutrons was Experimentally Found. In Case of Co-58
The Interference was Found to be Up to 6% For the
Lowest Cross Sections.
Uncertainty in the Proton Energy Including
Errors in the Thickness of Target Foils and Energy
Straggling.
The Cross Sections Given Include Errors
In Current Integration, Counting Statistics and
Photopeak Integration, Detector Efficiency, Target
Thickness and Half-Life. Not Included are the Errors
In Absolute Branching Ratios. However, Inconsistencies
In the Branching Ratios are Considered in Those Cases
In Which Several Undisturbed Gamma-Rays were Available.
The Experimental Values were Compared to Compound/
Precompound Calculations Using the Blann Code 'Overlaid
Alice'.
Lapf
Some Corrections have Been Included by
CAJAD
Reaction Lines are Corrected.
Last checking has been done.
- By Kachapag -
Main  Reaction  is (2N+P), But Cross Sections Below the
Threshold of the (P,2N+P) Reaction are Due to the
Reactions (P,N+D) And (P,T), Respectively.  Above 35
MeV a Small Contribution of Ni-60(P,2N+T)Ni-56 is
Possible.
Cu-56 Production is Poosible Too at Highest Energy.
- By Kachapag -
Above 25 MeV a Small Contribution of Ni-60(P,N+T)Ni-57
Is Possible.
The Uncertainty of Co-56 Production Cross Section is
Large Than Ni-56 Cross Section. Therefore this Cross
Section Could be Assumed As Independent - Comment of
CAJAD.
Shielded
100% Isomeric Transition;  Comment
By the Compiler of Kachapag.
The Deposit of Co-60 Production Due to Fe-60 Decay is
Negligible.
99.8% Isomeric Transition;
Comment by the Compiler of Kachapag.
.2%  Isomeric Transition -Comment by
The Compiler of Kachapag-
